extraction_reasoning: The litigation tables distinguish matters against the Company, Directors, Promoters and Group Companies from matters by those parties; I counted only against rows. Criminal proceedings against total 105 (Company 1, Director 2, Promoter 2, Group Company 100), statutory/regulatory actions against total 4 (Company 4), and tax proceedings against the Company and subsidiaries total 7 (Company direct tax 2 plus indirect tax 5); all “by” rows were excluded. The quantified litigation amount against the Company and subsidiaries is the sum of the Company’s against amounts: ₹1.02 million criminal + ₹271.39 million civil + ₹46.43 million direct tax + ₹99.20 million indirect tax + ₹3.89 million regulatory = ₹421.93 million, or ₹42.193 crore; no subsidiary amount was disclosed. The contingent-liabilities figure is the stated Total of ₹1,881.00 million as at March 31, 2018, converted to ₹188.10 crore rather than re-summed. No company-wide top-five or top-ten customer concentration percentage is disclosed; the percentages of interest on portfolio loans are not customer concentration figures. The source tables use ₹ million, converted to crore at 1 crore = ₹10 million.
